_README: Template manifest. Replace the placeholder URLs with your own image-editing comparisons, hosted somewhere the crowd can fetch (e.g. assets.rapidata.ai or any public bucket). Every pair needs a unique file name. 'input' (the source image shown to the annotator) is optional but strongly recommended for editing tasks - include it on ALL pairs or NONE. truth = 'a' or 'b' for trap/gold; null for real.
name: Image-edit audience quality diagnostic
instruction: You are shown the ORIGINAL image and two edited versions. Which edited image more accurately applies the requested change while leaving everything else unchanged?

pairs.edit: Remove the person standing on the left.
pairs._note: trap = the wrong option is OBVIOUSLY broken (garbled, blank, untouched). Anyone paying attention gets this right.

pairs.edit: Change the car colour to red.
Remove the coffee cup from the table.
pairs._note: gold = the wrong option is PLAUSIBLE but clearly worse to an attentive human (edit not actually done, wrong object, artifacts, identity drift). This is the real test of edit-quality perception.

pairs.edit: Add a hat to the dog.
Replace the text on the sign with 'OPEN'.
Change the season to winter with snow.
pairs._note: real = genuine model-vs-model edit, no known answer. Measures consensus strength and split-half reliability.

pairs.edit: Make the photo look like a watercolour painting.
